title: CONCEPT.md
description: Schema for a game concept document. The Concept Generator must produce output that satisfies this. Each property maps to an H2 section in the markdown file.

game_summary.description: One paragraph. What the game is — genre, setting, player action, win condition. Should be intelligible to someone who has never heard of it. No bullet points.

core_loop.description: The moment-to-moment player experience. What the player does, what happens as a result, what the feedback is. Controls, scoring, failure, and win state must all be covered. Written as prose or tight bullets — not a feature list.

target_feel.description: The emotional and kinesthetic quality the game should have. Genre references, tone words, what 'success' feels like to the player. Guides art, audio, and physics tuning decisions.

scope_constraints.description: Explicit in/out decisions for v1. Each item is a constraint — a feature that is definitively in scope or definitively out. No maybes. The Director and all agents treat these as hard rules.

items.description: A single scope decision, e.g. 'No audio — out of scope for v1' or 'Shooting is in scope — X fires a bullet'.

known_unknowns.description: Decisions not made here — explicitly deferred to a specific agent. Each item names the decision and who owns it. Optional — omit only if every decision is made.

deferred_to.description: Role id of the agent who will make this call (e.g. 'dev', 'art', 'level').
